target_problem: reduce unintended NPC actions and manual rule adjustment under scenario shift
scientific_setting: single-agent game environments with partially observed state and changing scenario rules
intended_claim: an uncertainty-triggered, action-constrained residual policy improves robustness and reduces adjustment work without sacrificing task success
observed_failure: a reproduced behavior-cloning policy works on familiar scenes but emits invalid or intent-inconsistent actions after distribution shift
mechanism_hypothesis: shift raises policy uncertainty while the unconstrained action head has no explicit representation of scenario-valid actions
novelty_thesis: couple semantic action masking with uncertainty-triggered residual correction so the baseline is preserved in familiar states and changed only for high-risk decisions
why_not_simple_splice: the mask and residual policy address different mechanisms and are connected by an explicit uncertainty trigger rather than unconditional stacking
constraints: p95 decision latency increase below 15 percent
all source records are synthetic evaluation fixtures
